description: A customer identifier is interpolated directly into a SQL query, enabling SQL injection.
expected_fix_type: parameterized query with separately bound values

description: A synthetic JWT signing secret is hardcoded in source instead of being loaded from protected configuration.
The final inventory entry is omitted by an off-by-one range boundary.
expected_fix_type: include every quantity in the total

description: The free-shipping threshold uses a strict comparison and incorrectly charges orders exactly at the threshold.
A normalized country value is computed but ignored, producing inconsistent labels and leaving an unused variable.
expected_fix_type: use the normalized country in the returned label

description: Configuration parsing leaks low-level file and JSON exceptions instead of raising the documented domain-specific error.
expected_fix_type: catch expected load failures and raise ConfigError with exception chaining

description: Refund authorization ignores the actor role, so an administrator cannot refund another customer's order despite the documented policy.
expected_fix_type: authorize the owner or an actor with the administrator role
